name: webhook-fanout
description: Iterate an upstream array and POST each item to a target URL with a vault-resolved bearer-style auth header. Designed for the common Talos→external-app integration pattern (e.g. pa-followup-tracker dispatching tasks into a sister app). Continues on per-item failures by default; surfaces an error sample so transient issues don't silently drop items.

URL.description: Target URL. Each item is POSTed here. For worker→host-bridge use 'http://host.docker.internal:<port>/...' on Mac/Win Docker (requires WORKER_ALLOW_PRIVATE_HOST_TARGETS=true on the worker).

INPUT_FIELD.description: Top-level field on the upstream output to read the array from. e.g. if upstream emits {"stale": [...]} use 'stale'. If the field is absent OR the upstream payload itself is an array, the upstream is treated as the array directly.

AUTH_HEADER_NAME.description: Outbound header name carrying the auth token. Use 'authorization' if your target expects RFC 7235 Bearer.

AUTH_HEADER_VALUE.description: Token value. Set to 'vault://path/to/secret' so the worker resolves it server-side at fetch time — the plaintext NEVER enters guest WASM memory. Module's allowed_secrets MUST include the path.

EXTRA_HEADERS.description: Optional additional headers. content-type defaults to application/json and is overridable here.

TIMEOUT_MS.description: Per-request timeout. Total wall time for the node is bounded by node timeout_secs.

STOP_ON_ERROR.description: When true, abort the fan-out on the first per-item failure (returns the partial-dispatch summary as an Err). Default false: log + count errors and keep going so transient downstream issues don't drop the entire batch.

MAX_ITEMS.description: Hard cap on items dispatched per execution. Protects against runaway loops if upstream emits an unexpected shape.
